title: My First Snowman
summary: A young child in Quebec describes making her very first snowman with her dad on a cold Saturday morning.
body: Last Saturday morning I saw white snow on the garden. It was my first big snow. I was very happy. I ran to the window and shouted, 'Dad! Look! Snow!' My dad smiled and said, 'Put on your warm clothes. We will make a snowman.' I put on my red coat, my blue hat, and my thick gloves. The garden was very white. The snow was soft and very cold. We made three big balls. The first ball was for the body. The second was for the chest. The third was small. It was for the head. Dad lifted the last ball for me. We put a long carrot for the nose. We used two small stones for the eyes. I put my old blue scarf around its neck. Mum came outside with hot chocolate in two cups. We sat on the steps and drank our chocolate. My fingers were cold but my heart was warm. I called the snowman Pierre. Pierre is my first friend made of snow.

annotations.exampleEn: There is white snow on the trees.
The children made a big snowman.
Put on your warm clothes before you go out.
She wore thick gloves in the cold wind.
We rolled three big balls of snow.
The first ball was for the body.
The second ball was for the chest.
Dad lifted the heavy box for me.
We used two small stones for the eyes.
She put a long scarf around her neck.
On cold days I love hot chocolate.

grammarNotes.title: Ordinal numbers: first, second, third
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để nói thứ tự ta dùng các số thứ tự: 'The first ball was for the body. The second was for the chest. The third was small.' Một số thứ tự hay gặp ở A1: first (thứ nhất), second (thứ hai), third (thứ ba), fourth, fifth.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use ordinal numbers to give an order: 'The first ball was for the body. The second was for the chest. The third was small.' Common A1 ordinals: first, second, third, fourth, fifth.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple of common irregular verbs
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Một số động từ bất quy tắc xuất hiện trong bài: 'I saw snow' (see→saw), 'I ran to the window' (run→ran), 'Dad said' (say→said), 'We made three big balls' (make→made), 'Mum came outside' (come→came), 'we drank' (drink→drank). Học thuộc dạng quá khứ vì chúng không thêm '-ed'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Common irregular past forms in the text: saw (see), ran (run), said (say), made (make), came (come), drank (drink). Learn these by heart — they do not add '-ed'.

translationVi: Sáng thứ Bảy tuần trước, tôi thấy tuyết trắng phủ trên khu vườn. Đó là lần đầu tiên tôi thấy tuyết rơi nhiều. Tôi rất vui. Tôi chạy đến cửa sổ và hét lên: 'Bố ơi! Nhìn nè! Tuyết!' Bố cười và nói: 'Mặc đồ ấm vào. Hai bố con mình sẽ làm người tuyết.' Tôi mặc áo khoác đỏ, đội mũ xanh, đeo găng tay dày. Khu vườn trắng xóa. Tuyết mềm và rất lạnh. Chúng tôi nặn ba quả cầu lớn. Quả thứ nhất làm thân. Quả thứ hai làm ngực. Quả thứ ba thì nhỏ, để làm đầu. Bố nhấc quả cầu cuối cùng giúp tôi. Chúng tôi cắm một củ cà rốt dài làm mũi. Chúng tôi dùng hai viên đá nhỏ làm mắt. Tôi quàng cái khăn xanh cũ của mình lên cổ người tuyết. Mẹ ra vườn mang theo hai cốc sô-cô-la nóng. Chúng tôi ngồi trên bậc thềm và uống sô-cô-la. Ngón tay tôi lạnh cóng nhưng trong lòng thì rất ấm. Tôi đặt tên người tuyết là Pierre. Pierre là người bạn bằng tuyết đầu tiên của tôi.

questions.explanation: 'Last Saturday morning I saw white snow.'

options: She saw snow for the first time in a big way
questions.explanation: 'It was my first big snow. I was very happy.'
'Put on your warm clothes. We will make a snowman.'

options: A green coat and a black hat
A red coat, a blue hat, and thick gloves
A yellow coat and white gloves
A black coat and a red hat

questions.explanation: 'The third was small. It was for the head.'

questions.prompt: What did they use for the nose?

questions.explanation: 'I put my old blue scarf around its neck.'
'Mum came outside with hot chocolate in two cups.'

questions.prompt: What name did the child give the snowman?
What does the child mean by 'my fingers were cold but my heart was warm'?
options: She felt happy even though it was cold
She wanted to go back inside
questions.explanation: Her body was cold but she was happy with her family.

blanks.explanation: 'my old blue scarf around its neck.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why are some childhood memories, like a first snowman, so special? What is a small memory you want to keep?
